version_date: September 2015 (published September 2015; on gazettal it repealed Statement of Planning Policy 5.2 Telecommunications Infrastructure (2004))
scope_summary: Provides land use planning guidance for the location, siting and design of above- and below-ground telecommunications infrastructure (mobile base stations, NBN fixed wireless towers, pit and pipe) throughout Western Australia, other than facilities exempted under the Commonwealth Telecommunications Act 1997. It balances effective network roll-out against visual impacts, directs that health/safety EME setbacks and buffer zones must NOT be set in local schemes or policies, and guides how schemes, structure plans and development applications should treat telecommunications infrastructure.

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum infrastructure height for recommended development approval exemption
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: telecommunications infrastructure local governments should consider exempting from development approval

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum advertising period for development proposals
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: advertising of telecommunications infrastructure development proposals

cross_references.cited: Statement of Planning Policy 5.2 Telecommunications Infrastructure (2004) (repealed by this policy)
Mobile Phone Base Station Deployment Industry Code (C564:2011)

quality_flags: Two-column PDF extraction interleaves columns in places (e.g. TOC merged with disclaimer); substantive provisions readable.
Exact gazettal date not stated in extracted text; only the publication month (September 2015) is given.
